description_en:
Adam (peace be upon him) is the first human being and the first prophet sent by Allah. He was created from clay — Allah fashioned him with His own hands and breathed His spirit into him. When Allah commanded the angels to prostrate before Adam in honor of this creation, all obeyed except Iblis, who refused out of arrogance and was thus expelled from the mercy of Allah.

Allah says in the Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:30): "And when your Lord said to the angels: 'I am going to place a successive authority on earth.' They said: 'Will You place upon it one who causes corruption therein and sheds blood, while we declare Your praise and sanctify You?' Allah said: 'Indeed, I know that which you do not know.'" This verse establishes Adam's role as the first khalifah — a vicegerent entrusted with inhabiting and tending the earth.

Allah taught Adam the names of all things, a sign of the special knowledge and intellect granted to humanity above other creation. Adam and his wife Hawwa (Eve) were placed in a garden of Paradise and were permitted to enjoy all its fruits except one tree, which they were warned not to approach. Deceived by the whispering of Iblis, they ate from it. Recognizing their error, they turned in sincere repentance, saying — as recorded in the Quran (Surah Al-A'raf 7:23): "Our Lord, we have wronged ourselves, and if You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we will surely be among the losers."

Allah, the Most Merciful, forgave them. However, they were sent to earth as a test and a trial, with the promise that guidance would come. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:38): "We said: 'Go down from it, all of you. And when guidance comes to you from Me, whoever follows My guidance — there will be no fear concerning them, nor will they grieve.'"

On earth, Adam and Hawwa became the parents of all humanity. Adam continued to receive revelation and call his descendants to the worship of Allah alone. He lived a long life — classical scholars note he lived approximately 1,000 years — during which he established the foundations of human civilization, family, and worship. His two sons Habil (Abel) and Qabil (Cain) are mentioned in the Quran in connection with the first act of murder in human history (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:27–31).

Adam is honored in Islam as Abu al-Bashar — the father of humanity — and as a prophet who received direct revelation from Allah. His story is a lesson in the mercy of Allah, the danger of arrogance (as exemplified by Iblis), the importance of sincere repentance, and the dignity bestowed by Allah upon the human being.
description_id:
Adam (alayhis salam) adalah manusia pertama sekaligus nabi pertama yang diutus oleh Allah. Ia diciptakan dari tanah liat — Allah membentuknya dengan tangan-Nya sendiri dan meniupkan ruh-Nya ke dalamnya. Ketika Allah memerintahkan para malaikat untuk bersujud kepada Adam sebagai penghormatan atas penciptaan ini, semua malaikat taat kecuali Iblis, yang menolak karena kesombongannya dan akhirnya diusir dari rahmat Allah.

Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:30): "Dan ingatlah ketika Tuhanmu berfirman kepada para malaikat: 'Aku hendak menjadikan khalifah di bumi.'" Ayat ini menetapkan kedudukan Adam sebagai khalifah pertama — wakil yang dipercaya untuk menghuni dan menjaga bumi.

Allah mengajarkan kepada Adam nama-nama segala sesuatu, sebuah tanda ilmu dan kecerdasan khusus yang dikaruniakan kepada manusia melebihi makhluk lainnya. Adam dan istrinya Hawwa ditempatkan di surga dan diizinkan menikmati seluruh buahnya kecuali satu pohon. Terbujuk oleh bisikan Iblis, mereka memakan buah pohon itu. Menyadari kesalahan mereka, keduanya bertaubat dengan tulus, berdoa — sebagaimana tercatat dalam Al-Quran (Surah Al-A'raf 7:23): "Ya Tuhan kami, kami telah menzalimi diri kami sendiri. Jika Engkau tidak mengampuni kami dan merahmati kami, niscaya kami termasuk orang-orang yang rugi."

Allah Yang Maha Pengasih mengampuni mereka, namun mereka diturunkan ke bumi sebagai ujian, dengan janji bahwa petunjuk akan datang. Di bumi, Adam dan Hawwa menjadi orang tua seluruh umat manusia. Adam terus menerima wahyu dan menyeru keturunannya untuk menyembah Allah semata.

Adam dimuliakan dalam Islam sebagai Abul Bashar — bapak umat manusia — dan sebagai nabi yang menerima wahyu langsung dari Allah. Kisahnya mengajarkan tentang kasih sayang Allah, bahaya kesombongan seperti yang dicontohkan Iblis, pentingnya taubat yang tulus, dan kemuliaan yang Allah anugerahkan kepada manusia.

title_en: Prophet Nuh (Noah, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Nuh (peace be upon him) is one of the greatest prophets in Islam and is known by the title "the second father of humanity." He was sent by Allah to a people who had fallen into widespread idol worship, and he called them patiently and persistently to the worship of Allah alone for an extraordinary 950 years.

Allah says in the Quran (Surah Nuh 71:5-6): "He said: 'My Lord, I have called my people night and day. But my call only increased them in flight.'" Despite his tireless efforts — calling in public and in private, openly and in secret — only a small number believed. The vast majority mocked and rejected him, covering their ears and wrapping themselves in their garments to avoid hearing his message.

After centuries of preaching, Allah revealed to Nuh that no more of his people would believe, and He commanded him to build a great ark. Nuh built the ship under divine instruction, and whenever the chiefs of his people passed by, they mocked him. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Hud 11:38): "And whenever the chiefs of his people passed by him, they ridiculed him. He said: 'If you ridicule us, then we will ridicule you just as you ridicule.'" Nuh remained steadfast in his task.

When the command came, water gushed forth from the earth and fell from the sky. The Great Flood covered the entire earth, destroying all who had rejected the message of Allah. Nuh loaded onto the ark a pair from every species of animal, along with those who believed with him. One of the most poignant moments in the Quran is the drowning of Nuh's own son, who refused to board the ark and was swallowed by the waves (Surah Hud 11:42–43). Nuh called out to his Lord in grief, and Allah reminded him that the disbeliever was not among his true family — the bond of faith supersedes the bond of blood.

After the flood receded, the ark came to rest on Mount Judi. Nuh and his followers disembarked and repopulated the earth. Allah blessed Nuh and his descendants. The Quran says (Surah As-Saffat 37:77–78): "And We made his descendants the ones who survived. And We left for him favorable mention among later generations."

The story of Nuh is a profound lesson in patience, perseverance in calling to truth, the certainty of divine justice, and the reality that nearness of blood does not guarantee salvation — only faith and righteousness do. An entire surah of the Quran (Surah Nuh, 71) is named after him.
description_id:
Nuh (alayhis salam) adalah salah satu nabi terbesar dalam Islam dan dikenal dengan gelar "bapak kedua umat manusia." Ia diutus oleh Allah kepada suatu kaum yang telah terjerumus dalam penyembahan berhala secara meluas, dan ia menyeru mereka dengan sabar dan gigih untuk menyembah Allah semata selama 950 tahun.

Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran (Surah Nuh 71:5-6): "Dia berkata: 'Ya Tuhanku, sesungguhnya aku telah menyeru kaumku siang dan malam. Tetapi seruanku itu hanya menambah mereka lari.'" Meskipun berusaha tanpa henti — menyeru secara terang-terangan dan rahasia — hanya sedikit yang beriman. Kebanyakan mengejek dan menolaknya, menutup telinga dan menutup diri agar tidak mendengar pesannya.

Setelah berabad-abad berdakwah, Allah mewahyukan kepada Nuh bahwa kaumnya tidak akan beriman lagi, dan memerintahkannya membangun sebuah bahtera besar. Nuh membangunnya sesuai petunjuk ilahi, sementara para pemimpin kaumnya selalu mengolok-oloknya. Nuh tetap teguh dalam tugasnya.

Ketika perintah datang, air memancar dari bumi dan turun dari langit. Banjir besar menenggelamkan seluruh bumi, menghancurkan semua yang telah menolak risalah Allah. Salah satu momen paling menyentuh dalam Al-Quran adalah tenggelamnya putra Nuh sendiri yang menolak naik ke bahtera (Surah Hud 11:42-43). Allah mengingatkan Nuh bahwa orang kafir bukanlah bagian dari keluarganya yang sejati — ikatan iman melampaui ikatan darah.

Setelah banjir surut, bahtera berlabuh di Bukit Judi. Kisah Nuh adalah pelajaran mendalam tentang kesabaran, ketekunan dalam menyeru kebenaran, dan kepastian keadilan ilahi. Satu surah penuh dalam Al-Quran (Surah Nuh, 71) dinamakan menurut namanya.

title_en: Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Ibrahim (peace be upon him) holds one of the most exalted ranks among all the prophets and messengers. He is known by the title Khalil Allah — the intimate friend of Allah — a distinction mentioned explicitly in the Quran (Surah An-Nisa 4:125). He is also called Abu al-Anbiya — the father of the prophets — as the prophetic lineage of both Banu Isra'il (through his son Ishaq) and the Arabs (through his son Ismail) traces back to him, including the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.

Ibrahim was born into a family of idol-makers in the region of ancient Iraq. From his youth, he used his intellect and the sound nature (fitrah) to reject the worship of idols, stars, the moon, and the sun, declaring: "I have turned my face toward He who created the heavens and the earth, inclining toward truth, and I am not of those who associate others with Allah" (Surah Al-An'am 6:79). He confronted his father and his people with clarity and wisdom, and when they refused to listen, he smashed their idols and left the largest unharmed, challenging them to ask it. This act of tawhid infuriated the king Nimrod, who ordered Ibrahim thrown into a massive fire. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:68–69): "We said: 'O fire, be coolness and safety upon Ibrahim.'" He emerged unharmed — one of the greatest miracles recorded in the Quran.

Ibrahim then migrated across the ancient world. He was commanded to leave his wife Hajar and his infant son Ismail in the barren valley of Mecca — an act of trust in Allah that led, by divine provision, to the discovery of the spring of Zamzam and the establishment of what would become the holiest city on earth.

The greatest test of Ibrahim's life came when Allah commanded him in a dream to sacrifice his son. Both father and son submitted with complete willingness — Ibrahim raised the knife, and at that moment Allah substituted a ram. This supreme act of surrender is commemorated every year by Muslims around the world during Eid al-Adha. Allah says (Surah As-Saffat 37:104–105): "We called to him: 'O Ibrahim, you have fulfilled the vision.' Indeed, We thus reward the doers of good."

Ibrahim and his son Ismail later raised the foundations of the Ka'bah in Mecca, praying: "Our Lord, accept this from us. Indeed, You are the Hearing, the Knowing" (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:127). The Ka'bah remains the qiblah — the direction of prayer — for over a billion Muslims to this day.

The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ said: "The most beloved of names to Allah are Abdullah and Abdurrahman," and the religion of Islam itself is described in the Quran as the Millah Ibrahim — the way of Ibrahim — emphasizing that his legacy is the foundation of the final message.
description_id:
Ibrahim (alayhis salam) memegang salah satu kedudukan paling mulia di antara seluruh nabi dan rasul. Ia dikenal dengan gelar Khalilullah — kekasih Allah — sebuah kehormatan yang disebutkan secara eksplisit dalam Al-Quran (Surah An-Nisa 4:125). Ia juga disebut Abu al-Anbiya — bapak para nabi — karena garis kenabian Bani Isra'il (melalui putranya Ishaq) dan bangsa Arab (melalui putranya Ismail) semuanya kembali kepadanya, termasuk Nabi Muhammad ﷺ.

Ibrahim lahir dalam keluarga pembuat berhala di wilayah Irak kuno. Sejak masa mudanya, ia menggunakan akal dan fitrahnya untuk menolak penyembahan berhala, bintang, bulan, dan matahari. Ia menghadapi ayahnya dan kaumnya dengan jelas dan bijaksana. Ketika mereka menolak, ia menghancurkan berhala-berhala mereka. Perbuatan tauhid ini membuat Raja Namrud murka dan memerintahkan agar Ibrahim dilemparkan ke dalam api yang besar. Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:69): "Kami berfirman: 'Wahai api, jadilah dingin dan selamat bagi Ibrahim.'" Ia keluar tanpa cedera — salah satu mukjizat terbesar yang tercatat dalam Al-Quran.

Ibrahim kemudian diperintahkan meninggalkan istrinya Hajar dan bayinya Ismail di lembah Mekah yang tandus — sebuah ujian ketaatan kepada Allah yang berujung pada ditemukannya mata air Zamzam dan berdirinya kota tersuci di muka bumi.

Ujian terbesar Ibrahim datang ketika Allah memerintahkannya dalam mimpi untuk menyembelih putranya. Keduanya, ayah dan anak, menyerahkan diri dengan kerelaan penuh. Saat Ibrahim mengangkat pisau, Allah menggantikannya dengan seekor domba. Penyerahan diri yang agung ini diperingati setiap tahun oleh umat Muslim di seluruh dunia dalam Hari Raya Idul Adha.

Ibrahim dan putranya Ismail kemudian membangun kembali pondasi Ka'bah di Mekah, berdoa: "Ya Tuhan kami, terimalah amal ini dari kami." Ka'bah tetap menjadi kiblat bagi lebih dari satu miliar umat Muslim hingga hari ini.

title_en: Prophet Musa (Moses, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Musa (peace be upon him) is the most frequently mentioned prophet in the Quran, appearing in over seventy passages across dozens of surahs. He is one of the five great Messengers of firm resolve (Ulu al-'Azm) — alongside Nuh, Ibrahim, Isa, and Muhammad ﷺ — and his story contains some of the most detailed and powerful narratives in all of Islamic scripture.

Musa was born during the reign of Fir'awn (Pharaoh) in Egypt, at a time when the Banu Isra'il (Children of Israel) were enslaved and oppressed, and Fir'awn had decreed the killing of all newborn Israelite males. Allah inspired Musa's mother to place him in a basket on the river, and the infant was carried by the current directly into the palace of Fir'awn, where he was raised by Fir'awn's own household. This was divine providence — the very man who sought to destroy the Israelites was raising their deliverer in his own home. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Ta-Ha 20:39): "And I cast upon you love from Me that you would be brought up under My eye."

After inadvertently causing the death of an Egyptian man during a confrontation, Musa fled to Madyan, where he lived for years, married, and worked as a shepherd. On his return journey, he received his prophethood at the blessed valley of Tuwa when Allah spoke to him directly from a burning bush — one of the most celebrated theophanies in all of prophetic history. He was given two great signs: his staff that could turn into a serpent, and his hand which could become radiantly white. Allah commanded him: "Go to Fir'awn — indeed, he has transgressed" (Surah Ta-Ha 20:24).

Musa and his brother Harun (Aaron) confronted Fir'awn and his sorcerers. When the sorcerers cast their ropes and staffs to create the illusion of serpents, Musa's staff swallowed them all. The sorcerers — recognizing true miracle over mere trickery — fell in prostration and declared their faith, even as Fir'awn threatened them with death. This moment is among the most dramatic in the Quran.

Fir'awn and his people rejected the message despite nine clear miracles sent upon Egypt: the staff, the white hand, storms, locusts, lice, frogs, blood, drought, and loss of crops (Surah Al-A'raf 7:133). Finally, Allah commanded Musa to lead the Israelites out of Egypt by night. When Fir'awn's army pursued them to the sea, Allah commanded Musa to strike the water with his staff. The sea parted into twelve dry paths, the Israelites crossed to safety, and the sea closed over Fir'awn and his army. Allah preserved Fir'awn's body as a sign for future generations (Surah Yunus 10:92).

Musa received the Torah (Tawrah) on Mount Sinai in direct speech with Allah — an honor that earned him the title Kalim Allah, the one to whom Allah spoke. He led the Israelites through the wilderness for forty years, during which they were tested repeatedly with patience, gratitude, and obedience. His story is a timeless lesson in trusting Allah when all worldly means seem exhausted.
description_id:
Musa (alayhis salam) adalah nabi yang paling sering disebutkan dalam Al-Quran, muncul dalam lebih dari tujuh puluh bagian di berbagai surah. Ia adalah salah satu dari lima rasul Ulul Azmi — bersama Nuh, Ibrahim, Isa, dan Muhammad ﷺ — dan kisahnya memuat beberapa narasi paling rinci dan kuat dalam seluruh kitab suci Islam.

Musa lahir di masa pemerintahan Fir'aun di Mesir, ketika Bani Isra'il diperbudak dan ditindas, dan Fir'aun telah menetapkan pembunuhan semua bayi laki-laki Bani Isra'il. Allah mengilhami ibu Musa untuk meletakkannya dalam sebuah peti di atas sungai, dan bayi itu terbawa arus langsung ke istana Fir'aun, di mana ia dibesarkan oleh keluarga Fir'aun sendiri — orang yang berusaha menghancurkan Bani Isra'il justru membesarkan pembebas mereka.

Setelah meninggalkan Mesir dan menetap di Madyan, Musa menerima kenabiannya di lembah suci Tuwa ketika Allah berbicara langsung kepadanya dari pohon yang terbakar. Ia diberi dua tanda besar: tongkat yang bisa berubah menjadi ular, dan tangannya yang bisa bersinar putih terang. Allah memerintahkannya: "Pergilah kepada Fir'aun — sesungguhnya dia telah melampaui batas" (Surah Ta-Ha 20:24).

Musa dan saudaranya Harun menghadapi Fir'aun dan para penyihirnya. Ketika para penyihir dijinakkan oleh mukjizat Musa, mereka bersujud dan menyatakan keimanan. Fir'aun dan kaumnya menolak meskipun telah dikirimkan sembilan mukjizat nyata. Akhirnya Allah memerintahkan Musa membawa Bani Isra'il keluar dari Mesir. Ketika pasukan Fir'aun mengejar mereka ke tepi laut, Allah memerintahkan Musa memukul air dengan tongkatnya. Laut terbelah, Bani Isra'il menyeberang dengan selamat, dan laut menutup kembali menenggelamkan Fir'aun beserta tentaranya.

Musa menerima Taurat di Bukit Sinai dalam percakapan langsung dengan Allah — kehormatan yang memberinya gelar Kalimullah. Kisahnya adalah pelajaran abadi tentang kepercayaan kepada Allah ketika semua sarana duniawi tampak habis.

title_en: Prophet Dawud (David, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Dawud (peace be upon him) was both a prophet of Allah and a mighty king of the Children of Israel. His story is a testament to the fact that divine favor, extraordinary gifts, and political authority can be combined with deep personal piety and constant remembrance of Allah.

Dawud's rise to prominence began on the battlefield when, as a young man among the Israelite forces, he stepped forward to face the fearsome giant warrior Jalut (Goliath). While the seasoned soldiers of the army were too afraid to confront Jalut, Dawud — armed with faith and a sling — struck Jalut and killed him. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:251): "So Dawud killed Jalut, and Allah gave him the kingdom and prophethood and taught him from that which He willed."

Allah blessed Dawud with an extraordinary combination of gifts unlike any other prophet. He was given the Zabur (Psalms), revealed scripture in which he praised Allah with words of profound beauty. His voice in dhikr and tasbih was uniquely powerful — the mountains and birds would join him in glorifying Allah. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Saba 34:10): "And We certainly gave Dawud from Us bounty. O mountains, echo with him, and the birds also." The wind would carry his voice across vast distances, and all of creation harmonized with his glorification.

Allah also made iron pliant and soft in his hands, teaching him the craft of weaving coats of armor — a practical blessing that protected warriors and demonstrated that prophethood and useful skill are not in conflict (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:80). Dawud used this craft to support himself and his family rather than rely on the treasury of the state, a model of principled self-sufficiency that the Prophet Muhammad ﷺ praised.

As a ruler and judge, Dawud was known for his wisdom and discernment. The Quran records a notable test in which two litigants came before him (Surah Sad 38:21–24) — a parable through which Allah tested his judgment. When Dawud recognized his error, he immediately fell in prostration and repented. Allah accepted his repentance: "We forgave him that, and indeed for him is nearness to Us and a good place of return" (Surah Sad 38:25).

Dawud fasted every other day — the most demanding voluntary fast — and prayed the first half of every night. The Prophet Muhammad ﷺ said: "The most beloved fast to Allah is the fast of Dawud, and the most beloved prayer to Allah is the prayer of Dawud" (Sahih al-Bukhari). He divided his night into portions for rest, worship, and personal reflection — a model of balanced devotion that the Prophet ﷺ highlighted for its spiritual excellence.

Dawud's son Sulayman (Solomon) succeeded him as both prophet and king, and together their reigns represent the pinnacle of the Israelite prophetic civilization.
description_id:
Dawud (alayhis salam) adalah seorang nabi Allah sekaligus raja yang perkasa dari Bani Isra'il. Kisahnya membuktikan bahwa karunia ilahi yang luar biasa dan kekuasaan politik dapat bersatu dengan ketakwaan pribadi yang mendalam.

Kemunculan Dawud dimulai di medan perang ketika sebagai pemuda di antara pasukan Bani Isra'il, ia melangkah maju menghadapi prajurit raksasa Jalut (Goliath). Sementara prajurit berpengalaman terlalu takut, Dawud — berbekal iman dan ketapel — menyerang Jalut dan membunuhnya. Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran (Surah Al-Baqarah 2:251): "Maka Dawud membunuh Jalut, dan Allah memberinya kerajaan dan kenabian serta mengajarkan kepadanya apa yang Dia kehendaki."

Allah menganugerahkan kepada Dawud gabungan karunia yang luar biasa. Ia diberi Zabur (Mazmur), kitab suci yang diwahyukan berisi pujian kepada Allah dengan kata-kata yang penuh keindahan. Suaranya dalam berdzikir dan bertasbih sangat istimewa — gunung-gunung dan burung-burung ikut bertasbih bersamanya. Allah berfirman dalam Al-Quran (Surah Saba 34:10): "Dan sesungguhnya Kami telah memberikan kepada Dawud karunia dari Kami. Wahai gunung-gunung, bertasbihlah bersamanya, dan demikian pula burung-burung."

Allah juga menjadikan besi lunak di tangannya, mengajarkan kepadanya kerajinan membuat baju besi — berkah praktis yang melindungi para pejuang. Dawud menggunakan kerajinan ini untuk menghidupi dirinya dan keluarganya tanpa bergantung pada kas negara, sebuah model kemandirian yang dipuji oleh Nabi Muhammad ﷺ.

Dawud berpuasa sehari dan berbuka sehari — puasa sukarela yang paling berat — dan shalat separuh malam. Nabi Muhammad ﷺ bersabda: "Puasa yang paling dicintai Allah adalah puasa Dawud, dan shalat yang paling dicintai Allah adalah shalat Dawud" (Sahih al-Bukhari). Putra Dawud, Sulayman, mewarisinya sebagai nabi dan raja, dan bersama-sama pemerintahan mereka mewakili puncak peradaban kenabian Bani Isra'il.

title_en: Prophet Sulayman (Solomon, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Sulayman (peace be upon him), son of the prophet Dawud, was granted by Allah a kingdom of unparalleled power and scope — one that has never been matched before or since. Yet he was simultaneously a deeply devout prophet who combined worldly authority with sincere servitude to Allah, and who prayed: "My Lord, forgive me and grant me a kingdom such as will not belong to anyone after me. Indeed, You are the Bestower" (Surah Sad 38:35). Allah accepted this prayer and gave him dominion over things no one else has ever been given.

Among the extraordinary gifts given to Sulayman was command over the wind, which traveled at his direction swiftly and reliably (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:81). The jinn were subjected to his authority — they built for him great structures, dived into the sea to retrieve treasures, and carried out whatever tasks he commanded (Surah Saba 34:12–13). Allah says in the Quran (Surah Sad 38:36–38): "So We subjected to him the wind blowing by his command, gently, wherever he directed; and also the devils — every builder and diver — and others bound together in chains."

Sulayman was also granted the remarkable ability to understand and speak the language of animals and birds. The Quran records one incident in vivid detail: as his vast army marched, he heard an ant warn its colony — "O ants, enter your dwellings that you not be crushed by Sulayman and his soldiers while they perceive not" — and Sulayman smiled, grateful to Allah for the ability to perceive what others could not (Surah An-Naml 27:18–19).

The story of Sulayman and the Queen of Sheba (Bilqis) is one of the most celebrated narratives in the Quran (Surah An-Naml 27:20–44). His hoopoe bird (hudhud) brought him news of a great queen who ruled a prosperous people but worshipped the sun instead of Allah. Sulayman sent her a message calling her to Islam. Before she could travel to him, Sulayman had her throne transported to his court in the blink of an eye through the agency of a man who possessed knowledge from the scripture. When Bilqis arrived and saw the throne and the magnificent court of Sulayman — including his crystal palace floor that she mistook for water — she recognized the truth and submitted to Allah along with Sulayman.

Sulayman oversaw the construction of Bayt al-Maqdis (the Temple in Jerusalem) — one of the great architectural and spiritual achievements of the prophetic era. The jinn labored under his direction on its construction. His death came while he was standing leaning on his staff in prayer or supervision; the jinn did not know he had died until a termite ate through the staff and his body fell (Surah Saba 34:14). This event underscored a divine lesson: had the jinn possessed knowledge of the unseen, they would not have continued in humiliating servitude.

Sulayman's story is a lesson in using worldly power and divine gifts in the service of Allah, maintaining gratitude for extraordinary blessings, and demonstrating the breadth of Allah's sovereignty over all of creation.
description_id:
Sulayman (alayhis salam), putra nabi Dawud, dianugerahi oleh Allah sebuah kerajaan dengan kekuasaan yang tak tertandingi — yang tidak pernah ada sebelumnya dan tidak akan ada sesudahnya. Namun ia sekaligus adalah seorang nabi yang sangat saleh yang menggabungkan kekuasaan duniawi dengan pengabdian yang tulus kepada Allah, dan yang berdoa: "Ya Tuhanku, ampunilah aku dan anugerahkan kepadaku kerajaan yang tidak layak dimiliki oleh siapa pun setelahku" (Surah Sad 38:35). Allah mengabulkan doa ini.

Di antara karunia luar biasa yang diberikan kepada Sulayman adalah kuasa atas angin yang melakukan perjalanan atas perintahnya (Surah Al-Anbiya 21:81). Para jin tunduk di bawah kekuasaannya — mereka membangun baginya bangunan-bangunan besar, menyelam ke laut untuk mengambil harta, dan melaksanakan tugas apa pun yang diperintahkan (Surah Saba 34:12-13).

Sulayman juga dianugerahi kemampuan luar biasa untuk memahami dan berbicara dalam bahasa binatang dan burung. Al-Quran mencatat satu peristiwa secara terperinci: saat pasukannya yang luas berbaris, ia mendengar seekor semut memperingatkan koloninya agar masuk ke dalam sarang — dan Sulayman tersenyum, bersyukur kepada Allah atas kemampuan merasakan apa yang tidak bisa dirasakan orang lain (Surah An-Naml 27:18-19).

Kisah Sulayman dan Ratu Saba (Bilqis) adalah salah satu narasi paling terkenal dalam Al-Quran (Surah An-Naml 27:20-44). Burung hudhud membawakan berita tentang seorang ratu yang memerintah kaum yang makmur namun menyembah matahari. Sulayman mengutus pesan yang menyerunya kepada Islam. Sebelum ia tiba, Sulayman meminta singgasananya dibawa ke hadapannya dalam sekejap mata. Ketika Bilqis melihat singgasananya dan istana Sulayman yang megah, ia menyadari kebenaran dan menyerahkan dirinya kepada Allah.

Sulayman mengawasi pembangunan Bayt al-Maqdis (Bait Suci di Yerusalem) — salah satu pencapaian arsitektur dan spiritual terbesar di era kenabian. Kisah Sulayman adalah pelajaran tentang penggunaan kekuasaan duniawi dan karunia ilahi demi mengabdi kepada Allah.

title_en: Prophet Isa (Jesus, peace be upon him)
description_en:
Isa ibn Maryam (Jesus son of Mary, peace be upon him) is one of the greatest prophets and messengers in Islam — a word and spirit from Allah, sent to the Children of Israel as the penultimate messenger before the final Prophet Muhammad ﷺ. His birth, mission, miracles, and ultimate fate are described in the Quran in remarkable detail, and an entire surah is named after his mother (Surah Maryam, 19).

Isa's birth was one of the greatest miracles in history. His mother Maryam was a chaste and devout woman, chosen above all the women of her time. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Aal Imran 3:42): "And when the angels said: 'O Maryam, indeed Allah has chosen you and purified you and chosen you above the women of the worlds.'" The angel Jibril appeared to her and announced that she would bear a son by the command of Allah alone, without a father. The creation of Isa without a father is compared in the Quran to the creation of Adam — both were extraordinary acts of divine will: "Indeed, the example of Isa to Allah is like that of Adam. He created him from dust; then He said to him, 'Be,' and he was" (Surah Aal Imran 3:59).

Allah gave Isa extraordinary miracles to confirm his prophethood. Among them: he spoke as an infant in the cradle to defend his mother's honor (Surah Maryam 19:29–33); he fashioned the form of a bird from clay and breathed life into it by Allah's permission; he healed the blind and the leper; and he raised the dead — all by the permission of Allah alone, as he consistently emphasized. Allah says in the Quran (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:110): "When you created from clay the likeness of a bird by My permission, then you breathed into it, and it became a bird by My permission; and you healed the blind and the leper by My permission; and when you brought forth the dead by My permission."

Isa was given the Injeel (Gospel) — divine revelation that called humanity to the worship of Allah alone, confirming the Torah while correcting the deviations that had crept into the religion of the Israelites. He warned against materialism, called for sincerity in worship, and foretold the coming of the final prophet — referring to him, in the Quran's account, as Ahmad (Surah As-Saff 61:6).

Islam firmly rejects the Christian doctrine of crucifixion and divinity. The Quran is explicit: "And for their saying, 'We killed the Messiah, Isa son of Maryam, the messenger of Allah' — they did not kill him, nor did they crucify him, but it was made to appear so to them" (Surah An-Nisa 4:157). Rather, Allah raised Isa to Himself — he is alive in the heavens and will return before the Day of Judgment to confirm the truth of Islam, break the cross, abolish the jizyah, and restore justice on earth. His return is among the major signs of the Hour.

Isa is honored in Islam as Ruh min Allah — a spirit from Allah — and as the Masih (Messiah), but he is a human prophet and servant of Allah, not divine, not a son of God. Allah says (Surah An-Nisa 4:171): "The Messiah, Isa son of Maryam, was no more than a messenger of Allah."
description_id:
Isa ibn Maryam (Yesus putra Maria, alayhis salam) adalah salah satu nabi dan rasul terbesar dalam Islam — kalimat dan ruh dari Allah, diutus kepada Bani Isra'il sebagai rasul terakhir sebelum Nabi Muhammad ﷺ. Kelahiran, misi, mukjizat, dan takdirnya digambarkan dalam Al-Quran secara sangat terperinci, dan satu surah penuh dinamai sesuai nama ibunya (Surah Maryam, 19).

Kelahiran Isa adalah salah satu mukjizat terbesar dalam sejarah. Ibunya Maryam adalah wanita yang suci dan saleh, dipilih di atas semua wanita di zamannya. Malaikat Jibril hadir kepadanya dan mengumumkan bahwa ia akan mengandung seorang putra atas perintah Allah semata, tanpa ayah. Penciptaan Isa tanpa ayah dibandingkan dalam Al-Quran dengan penciptaan Adam: "Sesungguhnya perumpamaan Isa di sisi Allah adalah seperti perumpamaan Adam" (Surah Aal Imran 3:59).

Allah menganugerahkan kepada Isa mukjizat-mukjizat yang luar biasa: ia berbicara sebagai bayi di buaian untuk membela kehormatan ibunya; ia membentuk burung dari tanah liat dan menghembuskan kehidupan ke dalamnya dengan izin Allah; ia menyembuhkan orang buta dan penderita kusta; dan ia membangkitkan orang mati — semuanya hanya dengan izin Allah semata (Surah Al-Ma'idah 5:110). Isa diberi Injil — wahyu ilahi yang menyeru manusia untuk menyembah Allah semata dan membenarkan Taurat. Ia juga menubuatkan kedatangan nabi terakhir, yang disebut dalam Al-Quran sebagai Ahmad (Surah As-Saff 61:6).

Islam dengan tegas menolak doktrin Kristen tentang penyaliban dan ketuhanan. Al-Quran menegaskan: "Mereka tidak membunuhnya dan tidak menyalibnya, tetapi yang demikian itu hanya kesamaran bagi mereka" (Surah An-Nisa 4:157). Sebaliknya, Allah mengangkat Isa kepada-Nya — ia hidup di langit dan akan kembali sebelum Hari Kiamat untuk menegakkan kebenaran Islam dan keadilan di bumi. Isa dihormati dalam Islam sebagai Ruhullah — ruh dari Allah — dan sebagai Al-Masih, namun ia adalah nabi dan hamba Allah yang manusiawi, bukan ilahi. Allah berfirman (Surah An-Nisa 4:171): "Al-Masih Isa putra Maryam tidak lain hanyalah seorang utusan Allah."
